Include all students and professors linked to a department."],"grain":"Per department (group by departments.id)","aliases":{"d.id":"department_id","d.name":"department_name","COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0)":"student_count","COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0)":"professor_count","CASE WHEN COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0)::numeric / p_cnt.professor_count END":"student_to_professor_ratio"},"output_fields":["department_id","department_name","student_count","professor_count","student_to_professor_ratio"]},"visual_requirements":{"chart_type":"bar","x_axis":"department_name","y_axis":"student_to_professor_ratio","title":"Student-to-Professor Ratio by Department"},"assumptions":["Tables and columns used were confirmed in the preflight: public.departments (id, name), public.students (id, department_id), public.professors (id, department_id).","To avoid double-counting when counting students and professors, the implementation should aggregate counts in each table by department (subqueries or separate aggregates) and then join to departments. Example safe pattern: LEFT JOIN aggregated student counts and aggregated professor counts to departments.","Departments with no professors will have professor_count = 0; the ratio is returned as NULL in that case (division-by-zero handled via NULLIF or CASE). If you prefer an alternative (e.g., infinity or a very large number), specify and we'll adjust.","department_name (departments.name) is used as the label on the x-axis. If departments.name is not unique or you prefer departments.code, request a change.","No additional filters (e.g., only active students/professors or by academic year) were requested. If you need filtering, specify the filter rules and we will add them.","The metric expressions in 'metrics' are descriptive; the actual SQL should follow the safe aggregation pattern described above. An example final SQL (recommended) is:

SELECT
  d.id AS department_id,
  d.name AS department_name,
  COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0) AS student_count,
  COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) AS professor_count,
  CASE WHEN COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0)::numeric / p_cnt.professor_count END AS student_to_professor_ratio
FROM public.departments d
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS student_count FROM public.students GROUP BY department_id
) s_cnt ON s_cnt.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS professor_count FROM public.professors GROUP BY department_id
) p_cnt ON p_cnt.department_id = d.id;"]}},"endedAt":1771846074345},"react-loop-input":{"payload":{"plan":{"data_requirements":{"metrics":["COUNT(*) FROM students GROUP BY students.department_id AS student_count","COUNT(*) FROM professors GROUP BY professors.department_id AS professor_count","student_count::numeric / NULLIF(professor_count, 0) AS student_to_professor_ratio"],"dimensions":["departments.id","departments.name"],"filters":["None (no temporal or status filters requested). data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  d.id AS department_id,
  d.name AS department_name,
  COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0) AS student_count,
  COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) AS professor_count,
  CASE WHEN COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0)::numeric / p_cnt.professor_count END AS student_to_professor_ratio
FROM public.departments d
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS student_count
  FROM public.students
  GROUP BY department_id
) s_cnt ON s_cnt.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS professor_count
  FROM public.professors
  GROUP BY department_id
) p_cnt ON p_cnt.department_id = d.id
ORDER BY d.name ASC;

sample.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

issues: Departments with professor_count = 0 produce NULL student_to_professor_ratio (division-by-zero handled) — those departments will not render a bar; consider displaying a special indicator or using an alternative (e.g., '∞' or a large number) if you want a visible bar.
If department names are not unique, using department_name on the x-axis can be ambiguous — consider using department_id or a unique code in the label.

The chart does not explicitly set a sort for the nominal x axis; the SQL orders by d.name ASC which likely yields the desired order, but you may want to set encoding.sort to guarantee ordering in the visualization.

result.sql:
SELECT
  d.id AS department_id,
  d.name AS department_name,
  COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0) AS student_count,
  COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) AS professor_count,
  CASE WHEN COALESCE(p_cnt.professor_count, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE COALESCE(s_cnt.student_count, 0)::numeric / p_cnt.professor_count END AS student_to_professor_ratio
FROM public.departments d
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS student_count
  FROM public.students
  GROUP BY department_id
) s_cnt ON s_cnt.department_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS professor_count
  FROM public.professors
  GROUP BY department_id
) p_cnt ON p_cnt.department_id = d.id
ORDER BY d.name ASC;
